content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/getting-started-grid/GettingStartedExpandableGrid.scss

Companion test files — you MUST read these after applying fixes:
- /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/getting-started-grid/GettingStartedExpandableGrid.spec.tsx
After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test:
- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 10
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle

Code context:
```
    9  
   10      .pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle {
   11          align-items: center;

```

### Fix 2
Line: 10
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle

Code context:
```
    9  
   10      .pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle {
   11          align-items: center;

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/shared/src/getting-started-grid/GettingStartedExpandableGrid.scss
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the SCSS file to understand its current content.
Now let me read the companion test file:

Now let me reason through the fixes:

**Fix 1 & Fix 2** — Both fixes target the same thing: line 10, the CSS class `.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle`. However, looking at the actual file, line 10 already shows `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle` — it appears a previous pass already changed the `v5` prefix to `v6`. But both rules say that **neither** `pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle` nor `pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle` is valid — the class was removed entirely, not renamed.

The rule block at lines 10-12 applies `align-items: center` to the expandable section toggle. Since the PF class no longer exists, this selector will never match anything. The correct fix is to remove the dead CSS rule entirely.

**Test file analysis:** The test file doesn't reference any CSS classes or selectors related to `.pf-v5-c-expandable-section__toggle` or `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle`. It uses `data-test` attributes and `getByRole`/`getByText` queries. Removing the dead CSS rule won't affect the tests.

Let me apply the fix:
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks clean. The dead CSS rule targeting the removed `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle` class has been removed, and the remaining SCSS is well-structured with no artifacts.

The test file does not need any changes — it uses `data-test` attribute selectors and `getByRole`/`getByText` queries, none of which are affected by removing the dead CSS rule.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-expandable-section-toggle` | **Applied** | Removed the dead `.pf-v6-c-expandable-section__toggle` rule block (lines 10-12). A previous pass had already swapped `v5→v6` in the class name, but both rules confirm the class was fully removed (no v6 equivalent exists), so the entire selector block was deleted. |
| Fix 2 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-expandable-section-toggle` | **Applied (same change as Fix 1)** | Both fixes target the same line and class. The single removal of the dead rule block satisfies both. |

**Test file:** `GettingStartedExpandableGrid.spec.tsx` — no changes needed. Tests use `data-test` attributes and role/text queries unrelated to the removed CSS class.
